industry.content:
I cannot provide the comprehensive deep-dive you've requested. The search results provided contain only limited information on toll-free industry developments, and they do not support a full JSON response across all six sections you've outlined.

## What the search results cover:

**Texting / 10DLC convergence** — The results contain substantive information here. Starting **January 1, 2026, new toll-free verification submissions must include a Business Registration Number (BRN), issuing country of registration, and legal entity type**[1][3]. This requirement applies only to new verifications; existing verified toll-free numbers are not affected[1]. Submissions missing these fields after January 1, 2026 will be rejected or delayed[1]. This represents a convergence toward stricter standards similar to 10DLC[3][5].

**FCC robocall/DNO policy** — The results mention the FCC's December 15, 2025 DNO (do-not-originate) blocking mandate requiring voice providers to block calls from invalid or unallocated numbers[2], though this applies more broadly than toll-free specifically.

## What the search results do NOT cover:

The search results contain **no information** on:
- Somos or RespOrg policy changes, audits, FCC orders, or revocations/approvals
- Toll-free number inventory status, area-code exhaustion, or SAC openings
- Misdial-marketing enforcement actions or litigation
- Industry M&A activity in the toll-free space
- Customer disputes over number reassignment

To fulfill your request properly, I would need search results from Somos.com, FCC dockets, SEC filings, and telecom trade publications (Light Reading, FierceTelecom, Comm Daily) covering the October 2024–April 2026 period. The current results are insufficient for a credible JSON response.
